description: Tallish male, estimated about 45 years, greying hair and good physical condition.
exact_wording_or_concise_paraphrase: Concise paraphrase of Dwyer external examination.
notes: Age estimate is medical impression, not identity proof.

description: Fingernails and feet appeared cared for; nails carefully trimmed.
notes: Do not infer occupation or social status.

description: No external markings of note recorded by Dwyer.
notes: Does not equal a complete trauma exclusion without full autopsy file.

description: Postmortem rigidity described as intense; deep lividity noted behind/above ears and neck.
notes: Supports time/body-position discussion but not exact place of death.

description: Several teeth were missing; Dwyer prepared a missing-teeth chart.
notes: Exact tooth notation remains visually unresolved.

description: Pupils were small, unusual/uneven in outline, and about the same size.
notes: Dwyer cautioned drug association is broad and not distinguishing.

description: Small patch of dried saliva was noted at the right of the mouth.
notes: Interpretation of timing/ability to swallow remains opinion.

description: Sand was in the hair but not in nostrils or mouth.
notes: Useful for scene/body-position assessment later.

description: Scalp, skull and brain were normal except small brain vessels were readily visible with congestion.
notes: Coroner also summarized brain congestion at p.7.

description: Pharynx was congested; gullet showed superficial whitening and a patch of ulceration.
notes: Exact mucosal description should be checked before quotation.

description: Stomach deeply congested with superficial redness and small submucosal haemorrhages.
notes: Observed pathology; poison interpretation separate.

description: Blood was mixed with food in the stomach.
notes: Dwyer said he considered it present before postmortem.

description: Kidneys, liver, lungs, spleen and brain/upper vessels were described with congestion or engorgement.
notes: Exact organ-by-organ language should be quoted from official copy only.

description: Heart was normal in size and otherwise normal; muscle was tough/firm; heart was if anything contracted.
notes: Hicks later relied on contracted-heart observation.

description: Spleen described as strikingly large and firm, about three times normal size.
notes: No final cause inferred.

description: Food in stomach estimated by Dwyer as present up to 3 or 4 hours before death, with anxiety caveat.
notes: Meal timing is uncertain and physiologically caveated.

description: Microscopy showed destruction of centres of liver lobules; otherwise congestion signs predominated.
notes: OCR damaged; Cleland later found liver section not explanatory.

description: Dwyer stated death was not natural and immediate cause was heart failure, but he could not state what caused heart failure.
notes: Do not convert to final cause of death.

description: Dwyer initially suggested a barbiturate or soluble hypnotic, then accepted Cowan's negative finding while preserving possibility language.
notes: Hypothesis by medical witness, not detected substance.

description: Dwyer saw no evidence of hypodermic needle use; two recent abrasions on right hand did not appear significant.
notes: A tiny injection mark could still be missed per later witnesses.

description: Legs were sunburned to the crotch; trunk was not sunburned; Dwyer attributed burn to previous season or older.
notes: Do not infer residence/travel from this alone.

description: Cowan received stomach contents, liver/muscle, urine and blood on 2 December 1948.
notes: Specimen chain from P.C. Sutherland; full lab file missing.

description: Cowan tested for common poisons and found no signs of any common poison in the submitted specimens.
notes: Not equivalent to no poison present.

description: Cowan identified cyanides, alkaloids, barbiturates and carbolic acid as among common poisons tested/considered.
notes: Exact list may not be exhaustive.

description: Cowan said if death were by poison, it would be a very rare poison; he thought natural causes more likely than poisoning.
notes: Conflicts with other medical witnesses' emphasis.

description: Lawson made a mould/cast of the body features on 7 June 1949; formalin embalming affected appearance.
notes: Relevant to death mask limitations.

description: Durham photographed and fingerprinted the deceased and circulated prints/photos to Australian states, New Zealand and overseas fingerprint bureaus.
notes: No identification resulted according to testimony; full circulation log unavailable.

description: Clothing, suitcase and bust were marked as exhibits and ordered kept in police custody.
notes: Current custody not established.

description: Cleland agreed with age range about 40-45 or 40-50 and noted clean nails, clean shoes and cared-for appearance.
notes: Observation after embalming; avoid status inference.

description: Cleland considered death not natural and probably poison, but accepted Cowan found no poison present.
notes: Opinion not final cause determination.

description: Cleland discussed lividity around ears/neck as perhaps surprising but explainable by head support.
notes: Interpretive; not proof of original death location.

description: Cleland discussed barbiturates, alkaloids, cyanide, curare/tubarine and injection possibilities but found no poison fully fit the circumstances.
notes: Considered substances only.

description: Bennett examined the body at 9:40 a.m. on 1 December and estimated death up to eight hours earlier, not more than eight hours.
notes: Estimate based on cursory look/rigor and cyanosis.

description: Hicks thought barbiturates and morphine were unlikely causes under the timing and pathology described.
notes: Chemical names in page are distorted; preserve category only.

description: Hicks suspected a drug group capable of stopping the heart in systole and difficult to detect by ordinary chemical tests.
notes: Specific group/member names are obscured; do not name without manual transcription.

description: Hicks stated body relocation would remove some time-of-death difficulties; this was framed as conditional.
notes: Scenario caveat, not finding.

description: Hicks stated convulsions may precede death and the 7 p.m. arm movement could have been convulsive.
notes: OCR/handwriting damage; use cautiously.

description: Hicks excluded diabetic coma/insulin on liver and other findings and stated oral route would act less rapidly than injection.
notes: Needs manual transcription before quotation.
